food_subgroup: Sushi — thin rolled sushi (hosomaki) with mayonnaise filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general sushi roll category (FOODON_00002694) but no specific hosomaki-with-mayonnaise entry. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers sushi as a category. No specific hosomaki-mayo sub-code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for hosomaki or sushi roll food products at this level of specificity.

main_ingredients: filling (typically imitation crab / kani, cucumber, or tuna)
allergens: gluten (nori seasoning, soy sauce in rice seasoning)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Hosomaki Mayo is 8 pieces, each approximately 20 g, totalling approximately 160 g, as sold at Sushi Express Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sushi roll, with fish, USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — adapted with mayonnaise component adjustment for hosomaki-mayo profile
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entry for Japanese-style mayonnaise (Kewpie type)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Hosomaki Mayo is a thin sushi roll (nori, vinegared rice, mayonnaise filling). No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for hosomaki-mayo specifically. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Sushi, with fish' (FDC ID 175168) as the rice-nori base, with fat and energy adjusted upward to reflect the addition of Japanese mayonnaise (Kewpie-style, ~70 g fat per 100 g) at approximately 8–10% of total weight. Protein adjusted slightly downward relative to fish-heavy sushi. Sodium reflects sushi rice seasoning and nori. All values represent a reasonable per-100 g estimate for a mayonnaise-filled thin sushi roll.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sushi rice / vinegared rice analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for hosomaki-mayo. GI of 55 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sushi (vinegared white rice rolls), which reports GI values in the range 48–55. According to the standard classification, a GI of 55 is considered low. The addition of mayonnaise (high fat, negligible carbohydrate) slightly lowers the effective GI of the meal by slowing gastric emptying; however, the dominant glycemic driver remains the vinegared sushi rice. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 47.2) / 100 ≈ 26.0, where carbohydrate per 160 g serving = 29.5 × 1.6 = 47.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from sushi rice seasoning and nori
hypertension.guidance: Each 160 g serving of Hosomaki Mayo provides approximately 496 mg sodium (~22% of the 2,300 mg daily limit); individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid additional soy sauce dipping.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index but high glycemic load from vinegared sushi rice
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of approximately 55 but a high glycemic load of ~26 per serving, Hosomaki Mayo can still cause a significant postprandial glucose rise due to its carbohydrate content; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to half a serving and pair with non-starchy vegetables or protein to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with favourable unsaturated fat profile from Japanese mayonnaise
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hosomaki Mayo contains approximately 6.5 g fat per 100 g, predominantly unsaturated (from rapeseed-oil-based Japanese mayonnaise), with low saturated fat (~1 g per 100 g), making it a relatively heart-friendly choice when consumed in moderate portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hosomaki Mayo is a Japanese thin sushi roll (hosomaki) filled with Japanese mayonnaise and a complementary filling such as imitation crab (kani), cucumber, or tuna, wrapped in a single sheet of nori (dried seaweed) and rolled around vinegared short-grain sushi rice. Each roll is sliced into 6–8 bite-sized cylindrical pieces approximately 2–3 cm in diameter. The texture combines the slight chew of nori, the soft stickiness of vinegared rice, and the creamy richness of Japanese-style mayonnaise (Kewpie type), which is characterised by its tangy, umami-forward flavour from rice vinegar and MSG. Hosomaki Mayo is a popular menu item at conveyor-belt sushi restaurants (kaiten-zushi) across Japan and Singapore, including chains such as Sushi Express. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy food (~195 kcal per 100 g) with carbohydrate as the dominant macronutrient (~29.5 g per 100 g from sushi rice), moderate fat (~6.5 g per 100 g from mayonnaise), and modest protein (~4.8 g per 100 g). Sodium is moderate (~310 mg per 100 g) due to rice seasoning and nori. The glycemic index is approximately 55 (low), driven by the vinegared white rice base. Clinically, portion control is advised for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The mayonnaise component contributes predominantly unsaturated fats, which are relatively favourable for cardiovascular health.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore or USDA entry exists for hosomaki-mayo as a named dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy sushi-with-fish data with a mayonnaise component adjustment. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for vinegared sushi rice rolls. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy sushi source for this preparation. Vitamin B6 (vitamin_b6_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Vitamin B12 (vitamin_b12_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Phosphorus (phosphorus_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Magnesium (magnesium_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Zinc (zinc_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible in Japanese mayonnaise and sushi rice by USDA FoodData Central. Halal status is marked false as Japanese mayonnaise typically contains non-halal-certified ingredients unless specifically labelled; restaurant-level halal certification was not confirmed for Sushi Express Funan at time of processing. GI source is an analogous estimate; a direct GI measurement for hosomaki-mayo does not appear in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date.
